title: Ultimate Roleplay Kit - Hats
description: A resourcepack & datapack combo that adds a ton of cosmetic hats!
body:
**THIS DATAPACK/MOD REQUIRES A** [**DEPENDENCY RESOURCEPACK**](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/ultimate-roleplay-kit-resourcepack) **TO FUNCTION CORRECTLY!**

Ultimate roleplay kit (URK) is collection of datapacks, focused on adding vanilla friendly, multiplayer-focused features! So, items that don't add any innate gameplay, but can greatly enchance player interractions, which makes them a perfect addition to any vanilla-style server! 

This specific URK pack adds 36 diffirent cosmetic hats, that are sold by the Wandering Trader.

The mod version **still requires a resourcepack**

# Features:
![Cyan NoodleDoodle wearing a cyan-coloured Ushanka in front of a snowy backdrop](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/2da6831f5834d44aa1db332ae3cb90dfb1d275a9.png)
- **Hats** - Wandering traders now additionally sell 3 random hats from the datapack's pool. Each hat costs 4 emeralds + some a special items, to the specific hat (full list below). This is made in order to encourage exploration, as some of the items may be quite obscure.
- **Customisation** - Most hats can be dyed any colour, just like leather armour!
- **Locator Bar Integration** - All the hats also change your *locator bar icon*, and some may hide it alltogether.
- **Config Menu** A config menu can be called via this command: ```/function ndwthats:config_menu```. This menu allows you to configure the amount of diffirent hats sold per trader, and the locator bar settings.
- **Advancements** - An advancement tree including a brief tutorial, as well as some fun challenges for hat collectors out-there.

- ~~If you simply want to get all the hats right away, you can use the following command: ```/function ndwthats:giveallhats```~~ **Feature currently unavailable.**

URK items also include a few tooltip tags, which are supposed to signify what can be done with the items, to make the pack easier to use without a tutorial.
The following tags are used in this datapack:
- [H] **Headwear** - The item can be worn in your head slot, and will be displayed accordingly!
- [D] **Dyeable** - The item can be dyed any colour, just like leather armor!
- [E] **Emissive** - Some part, or the whole cosmetic glows in the dark. It does not emit light, or stop mobs from spawning, but the cosmetic item itself will be visible regardless of the light level.
- [S] **Stealth** - Hides your waypoint from the locator bar, if it's enabled.



<details>
<summary>Full list of cosmetics & trades</summary>

## Hats
Each hat requires 4 emeralds + some uniqie items to buy. Only the unique items are listed below. Each trade can only be done once per trader, and only 4 random ones are available at a time.
1. [H,D] **Duelist's hat:** 3 feathers
2. [H,D] **Bad boy cap:** 1 Flint and steel
3. [H,D] **Artistic berete:** 1 Brush

17. [H,D] **Winter ushanka:** 16 Snowballs

23. [H,D] **Scerene Flutterby** - 2 Honey Blocks

25. [H,D] **Deco Cake** - 1 Cake

28. [H,D] **Cute Bow** - 12 String

## Locator bar
![An example of the locator bar changes.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/f3c0e7c92f9cc1ab604cab9ce9f01a42ebfa0b86.png)

Wearing a hat changes ones locator bar icon (but not the colour). Some hats *hide* your icon from the locator bar, and are marked by the **Stealth** tag. By default these fetures apply to players only, but can be changed in the settings menu - ```/function ndwthats:config_menu```.

## Advancements

![Replace this with a description](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/b9b3ba46ef2a4314821e72dced6bc0ad95eb747a.png)

A few advancements were added to serve as an in-game tutorial to the datapack, so the players don't have to go online for the information on the pack. In addition, there are a couple of fun challenge advancements. These provide no EXP reward.

**To unlock the advancement tree, you need to pick up a single emerald.**

## How to install

In order to access the cosmetic items, you will have to install **both the datapack & the resourcepack!** The resourcepack adds the models & textures themselves, while the datapack makes the cosmetic items actually accessible in-game by adding them to the wandering trader's pool! **THIS IS STILL THE CASE IF YOU'RE USING THE MOD VERSION INSTEAD OF THE DATAPACK**
<details>
<summary>Resourcepack installation</summary>

- First of all, download the [resourecpack](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/ultimate-roleplay-kit-resourcepack) version appropriate for the datapack version. This can be checked in the specific version's changelog. drag the downloaded files into your ```.minecraft/resourcepacks``` folder, and then enable it via the in-game menu.

</details>





<details>
<summary>Datapack installation</summary>

  - To install the datapack, you will need to do one of the following:
- If you're creating a new world: in the world creation screen, naviagate to the "More" tab. Find & press the "Data packs" button, and then press "Open Pack Folder" in the new menu. Drag the downloaded file into this folder. After that, enable the newly added datapack via the in-game menu.
- If you're adding the pack to an existing world: drag the downloaded files into your ```.minecraft/saves/[WorldName]/datapacks``` folder, then re-enter the world. You may need to use the /datapack enable [pack_name] command, to activate the datapack.


</details>




<details>
<summary>Removing the packs</summary>

- In order to remove the Ultimate Roleplay kit from your world, you can simply delete the datapacks located in the ```.minecraft/saves/[WorldName]/datapacks``` folder. Any newly spawned wandering traders will not include the custom trades. The cosmetic items themselves will stay, and can be destroyed. Removing the resourcepack will also remove the cosmetic's custom models & textures


</details>

- When updating to a newer version of the Ultimate Roleplay Kit, please update both the resourcepack **AND** the datapacks! So don't forget to also download the resourcepack.




## Knowns issues & Planned features
Issues:
- Hats containing transparent elements may display incorrectly if you're using the **[3D skin layers mod](https://modrinth.com/mod/3dskinlayers)**
- This datapack may conflict with other ones that change the locator bar icons. In order to avoid any conflicts, open the settings menu via ```/function ndwthats:config_menu``` > Waypoint Display Mode > Affected Entities > None.

Plans: 
- More hats will be added from time to time, depending on my availability.
- ✅ **Implemented** ~~Locator bar support **will be** added as it comes to an official release.~~
- ✅ **Implemented** ~~Some sort of Settings/Options system to further customise the datapack for your needs.~~
- ✅ **Implemented** ~~Implementing minecraft's translate strings, to allow localisations.~~


## Other URK Packs:
### Mandatory Resourcepack:
- [Ultimate roleplay kit - Resourcepack](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/ultimate-roleplay-kit-resourcepack) - A mandatory dependency resourcepack, utilized by all other Datapacks.

### Content Datapcks:
- **Ultimate roleplay kit - Hats** - Adds 36 cosmetic hats, that are sold by the wandering trader. 
- [Ultimate roleaply kit - Mail](https://modrinth.com/datapack/ultimate-roleplay-kit-mail) - Adds 36 postel item designs, that can also be customised further with 11 unique seals.
